title: Hierochloe hirta
intro: Hierochloe hirta, also known as sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to North America and Eurasia, belonging to the Poaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Hierochloe hirta include essential oils, such as beta-phellandrene and beta-caryophyllene, as well as phenolic compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids. In traditional herbal medicine, Hierochloe hirta is commonly prepared as a tea, infused in water to release its aromatic oils, or used in smudging rituals to purify and protect the mind, body, and spirit.
taxonomy: Hierochloe hirta, also known as sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to the Northern Hemisphere. It belongs to the family Poaceae and is classified as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Angiospermophyta, Class: Liliopsida, Order: Poales, Family: Poaceae, Genus: Hierochloe, and Species: H. hirta.
